summary: In electrophoresis, most lipoproteins migrate based on their charges but chylomicrons remain stationary because they contain little protein; hence, chylomicrons are the only major lipid particle without significant mobility under these conditions. The absence of a net charge allows chylomicrons to stay at one spot during testing while other particles move accordingly depending upon their composition’s inherent properties influencing conductivity within such tests designed specifically around differential detection mechanisms exploiting differences between various biomolecules involved including those present even minimally
